# Restore ADR-19 compliance to required-config validation + promote the read-gate to a critical gate (#14499 regression)

## Incident

The orchestrator's Neural Link bridge crashed on restart after a `dev` pull:
`TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'mode')` at `ai/scripts/setup/initServerConfigs.mjs:741` (`aiConfig.auth.mode`).

Root cause: **#14499 (`ce09f82b1d`) shipped ‚â•2 ADR-19 violations, approved past two reviews** (@neo-opus-grace flip + @neo-opus-ada re-approve ‚Äî the rubber-stamp failure ADR-19 ¬ß3 D/E documents).

## The two antipattern classes (V-B-A'd; full-file signature audit found NO others ‚Äî no B3/B4/A1/A4)

1. **Hierarchy non-participation (the crash).** `assertConfigFresh` re-derives `aiConfig.auth.mode`, but `auth` is a **Tier-1-only** leaf. Server configs that don't import the Tier-1 realm root ‚Äî `neural-link`, `github-workflow`, `gitlab-workflow` (only `memory-core` does by the grep pattern; **KB nesting TBV with @neo-opus-grace**) ‚Äî have no resolvable `auth` ‚Üí crash. It also duplicates `validateRequiredEnv`'s own internal resolution (`mode ?? getData('auth.mode')`, `ConfigProvider.mjs:204`).
2. **Pass-along / B5 (¬ß3).** Daemons thread the SSOT in ‚Äî `assertConfigFresh({aiConfig: AiConfig})` at `orchestrator:201`, `kb-gc:51`, `kb-alerting:49`, `kb-reconciliation:51`; `BaseServer.mjs:542` threads `this.aiConfig`. `assertConfigFresh` is a **non-entrypoint**, so **C1** forbids it importing `AiConfig` ‚Äî the read must move to the entrypoints (¬ß5.5).

## Fix

- [x] **Crash fix** ‚Äî drop the `aiConfig.auth.mode` re-derivation; let `validateRequiredEnv` resolve mode via the hierarchy. Stale mock made faithful to the real Provider. **42/42 green.**
- [x] **Prevent recurrence** ‚Äî AGENTS.md **gate 10**: *"No AiConfig work without reading ADR-0019 first"* promoted into ¬ßcritical_gates (soft ¬ßedge_case trigger ‚Üí hard gate). Under the 24KiB cap.
- [ ] **Pass-along B5** ‚Äî move `validateRequiredEnv` to the entrypoints; `assertConfigFresh` becomes drift-only OR takes an injected `requiredFindings` value (Shape 1 vs Shape 2 ‚Äî @neo-opus-grace's ADR call).
- [ ] **Close the lint gap** ‚Äî neither hierarchy-participation nor pass-along-into-a-non-entrypoint is in the ADR-19 lint subset; that's why #14499 shipped green.

## Acceptance

- No server/daemon boot crashes on `auth.mode` resolution.
- No `aiConfig`/`AiConfig` threaded into `assertConfigFresh` (non-entrypoint).
- The ADR-19 read-gate is a critical gate; the lint gap is closed or ticketed.

Related: #14499, #13432, ADR-0019 (#12457).

- 2026-07-03T01:37:31Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `44a9754` - "fix(ai): server configs participate in the Tier-1 hierarchy + stop threading AiConfig through the boot guard (#14523)

Completes the #14499 ADR-19 remediation (participation + pass-along).

PARTICIPATION (2.1 / SSOT): neural-link, github-workflow, gitlab-workflow config.template.mjs now
load the Tier-1 realm root (import '../../../config.template.mjs', materialized to ../../../config.mjs)
like memory-core/knowledge-base, so getParent() resolves auth.* and shared leaves via the SSOT chain.
Without it aiConfig.auth.mode had no root -> the neural-link bridge boot crash.

DRIFT-DETECTION GAP (why it shipped stale): projectSourceShape only tracked `import ... from`; bare
side-effect imports were invisible, so a template ADDING a participation import was not detected as
drift and the overlay booted non-participating. Now tracked -> --migrate-config materializes the
import into config.mjs (+ a regression test).

NO PASS-ALONG (2/3, B5): assertConfigFresh is a non-entrypoint (ADR-19 C1) - it no longer takes
aiConfig and never reads the SSOT. Each ENTRYPOINT reads its own config at the use site
(config.validateRequiredEnv) and injects the computed requiredFindings value (the 5.5 narrow bootstrap
boundary), across all 11 boot guards (orchestrator + 4 kb/wake daemons, 5 mcp-server, run-bridge).

Verified by running: re-materialize -> the neural-link bridge boot guard passes (no auth.mode crash);
42/42 initServerConfigs specs green (incl. the new bare-import drift test)."
- 2026-07-03T01:37:31Z @neo-opus-ada referenced in commit `7539b7f` - "docs(agents): promote the ADR-0019 read-gate to critical gate 10 (#14523)

The ADR-19 read-gate lived in the edge-case triggers as a soft trigger that agents
reason past under task momentum (#14499 shipped >=2 ADR-19 violations past two reviews).
Promote it into the critical gates as gate 10 - a hard, no-exception gate - and drop the
demoted edge-case entry. Net byte-neutral; AGENTS.md stays under the 24KiB cap (24548/24576)."
- 2026-07-03T01:42:24Z @neo-opus-grace cross-referenced by PR #14529
- 2026-07-03T05:05:08Z @tobiu referenced in commit `6867e96` - "fix(ai): ADR-19 remediation ‚Äî assertConfigFresh crash fix + promote ADR-0019 read-gate to gate 10 (#14523) (#14524)

* fix(ai): stop re-deriving auth.mode in assertConfigFresh ‚Äî ADR-19 + NL-bridge boot crash (#14523)

assertConfigFresh re-derived `mode ?? aiConfig.auth.mode`, which (1) duplicates
validateRequiredEnv's own internal resolution (`mode ?? getData('auth.mode')`,
ConfigProvider.mjs:204) and (2) crashes on a server config whose chain has no
resolvable auth leaf ‚Äî the Tier-1-only auth block is absent from configs that do
not import the realm root (e.g. the neural-link bridge). Pass mode through and let
the Provider resolve it via the hierarchy.

The spec mock encoded the same antipattern (echoed the passed mode instead of
resolving it); made faithful to the real Provider. 42/42 green.

* fix(ai): server configs participate in the Tier-1 hierarchy + stop threading AiConfig through the boot guard (#14523)

Completes the #14499 ADR-19 remediation (participation + pass-along).

PARTICIPATION (2.1 / SSOT): neural-link, github-workflow, gitlab-workflow config.template.mjs now
load the Tier-1 realm root (import '../../../config.template.mjs', materialized to ../../../config.mjs)
like memory-core/knowledge-base, so getParent() resolves auth.* and shared leaves via the SSOT chain.
Without it aiConfig.auth.mode had no root -> the neural-link bridge boot crash.

DRIFT-DETECTION GAP (why it shipped stale): projectSourceShape only tracked `import ... from`; bare
side-effect imports were invisible, so a template ADDING a participation import was not detected as
drift and the overlay booted non-participating. Now tracked -> --migrate-config materializes the
import into config.mjs (+ a regression test).

NO PASS-ALONG (2/3, B5): assertConfigFresh is a non-entrypoint (ADR-19 C1) - it no longer takes
aiConfig and never reads the SSOT. Each ENTRYPOINT reads its own config at the use site
(config.validateRequiredEnv) and injects the computed requiredFindings value (the 5.5 narrow bootstrap
boundary), across all 11 boot guards (orchestrator + 4 kb/wake daemons, 5 mcp-server, run-bridge).

Verified by running: re-materialize -> the neural-link bridge boot guard passes (no auth.mode crash);
42/42 initServerConfigs specs green (incl. the new bare-import drift test).

* docs(agents): promote the ADR-0019 read-gate to critical gate 10 (#14523)

The ADR-19 read-gate lived in the edge-case triggers as a soft trigger that agents
reason past under task momentum (#14499 shipped >=2 ADR-19 violations past two reviews).
Promote it into the critical gates as gate 10 - a hard, no-exception gate - and drop the
demoted edge-case entry. Net byte-neutral; AGENTS.md stays under the 24KiB cap (24548/24576).
